rulings.Case Title: CHATEAU DE LOUIS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. ERIC AU et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 17, 2008, line 2, DEFENDANT ALICE AU'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. THE SAMUELS' DECLARATION LACKS FOUNDATIONAL FACTS SUFFICIENT TO QUALIFY HIM TO OFFER EXPERT OPINIONS AS A FORENSIC ACCOUNTANT AND THE REFERENCES TO THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE BANKRUPTCY COURT DO NOT CONSTITUTE ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. PLAINTIFF HAS BEEN GIVEN TIME TO PROVE THESE CLAIMS AND HAS FAILED TO DO SO. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: THELEN REID BROWN RAYSMAN & STEINER LLP VS. ANIMAL PARTNERS LIMITED PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 17, 2008, line 3, PLAINTIFF THELEN REID BROWN RAYSMAN & STEINER LLP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. No opposition filed. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: MARY RUTH LADD VS. PLANKTOS INC A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 17, 2008, line 4, DEFENDANTS PLANKTOS INC., D2FUSION INC A CORPORATION, SOLAR ENERGY LTD A CORPORATION, AND NELSON SKALBANIA'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED IN PART WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AND OVERRULED IN PART. IT IS SUSTAINED AS TO SKALBANIA AS TO THE 3RD AND 7TH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE COMPLAINT FAILS TO PROVIDE A BASIS FOR THESE CLAIMS BASED ON THE CONDUCT ALLEGED THAT IS ATTRIBUTABLE TO SKALBANIA. IT IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE CORPORATE ENTITIES AS TO THE 5TH AND 6TH CAUSES OF ACTION. AS TO FRAUD, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE KNOWLEDGE OR RECKLESS DISREGARD. AS TO THE CONTRACT CLAIM, PLAINTIFF MUST CLARIFY WHETHER THE BREACH IS OF THE WRITTEN OR ORAL OR BOTH CONTRACTS. DENIED AS TO THE 7TH AND 8TH CAUSES OF ACTION. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: ELEANOR JENKINS VS. WAYNE BELL et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 17, 2008, line 5, DEFENDANT DON WARD'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. THE AMENDED COMPLAINT ADDRESSES THE ISSUES THAT WERE THE SUBJECT OF THE PRIOR DEMURRER. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: LELA MARTIN et al VS. JOHN STEWART COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 17, 2008, line 8, DEFENDANT JOHN STEWART COMPANY'S Motion To Strike Complaint IS GRANTED. THE MOTION IS NOT MOOT BY THE FILING OF AN AMENDED COMPLAINT. SIMMONS V. ALLSTATE INS. CO. (2001) 92 CAL.APP.4TH 1068, 1073-1074. DEFENDANTS HAVE DEMONSTRATED THAT THE CONDUCT ALLEGED IS PROTECTED BY CCP SECTION 425.16 FOR IT IS BASED ON THE UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION. FELDMAN V. PARK LANE ASSOCIATES (2008) 160 CAL.APP.4TH 1467. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF HAS A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING. EACH OF THE CAUSES OF ACTION IS PREMISED ON DEFENDANT'S UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION AND THAT IS PRIVILEGED CONDUCT. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: MARK THIERMAN et al VS. JOHN KELSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Re: Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 17, 2008, line 10, PETITIONER JOHN KELSON'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. THE MOTION IS GRANTED. THE ARBITRATOR CORRECTLY EXERCISED HER AUTHORITY UNDER LOPEZ V. MILLSAP (1992) 6 CAL.APP.4TH 1679, 1687 TO CORRECT A COMPUTATIONAL ERROR IN THE INITIAL AWARD. THE CORRECTED FINAL AWARD IS CONFIRMED. PETITIONER IS ENTITLED TO COSTS, INTEREST AT 10% FROM OCTOBER 17, 2008 AND REASONABLE ATTORNEYS IN CONNECTION WITH THIS PROCEEDING. =(302/PJM/JW)
